description_long: This object is a single slice of rustic bread, likely sourdough or a similar artisanal variety, with a thick, golden-brown crust and a light, airy interior. The crust appears slightly glossy and uneven, with some areas darker than others, suggesting a hand-baked process. The interior is pale beige with visible air pockets and a soft, spongy texture, characteristic of well-fermented bread. The slice is irregularly shaped, with one side more rounded and the other tapering to a thinner edge. There are no visible seeds, flour dusting, or decorative scoring on the crust. The bread looks fresh and ready to be eaten, suitable for use in sandwiches or as a side in an urban café or park setting.
description: A single, rustic slice of bread with a thick, golden-brown crust and a light, airy interior. The slice is irregularly shaped and has a soft, spongy texture.
description_view_0: The bread slice is shown from the side, highlighting the thick, golden-brown crust and the airy, pale interior.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the tapering edge and the rounded end of the slice, with the crust appearing slightly thicker.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the full length of the crust, showing its uneven, glossy surface and the flatness of the slice.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the other angled end, revealing the soft, porous interior and the crust's transition from thick to thin.
attribute_crust_pattern: The crust is thick, uneven, and golden-brown with a slightly glossy finish. There are no visible seeds, flour dusting, or decorative scoring, giving it a rustic, hand-baked appearance.
